description: Identify nucleotide polymorphisms and short indels from Illumina and Element data.
name: Variant calling

workflows.workflowDescription: Variant calling and consensus sequence generation for batches of Illumina PE sequenced viruses with uncomplicated and stable genome structure (like e.g. Morbilliviruses).
workflows.workflowName: Variant calling and consensus construction from paired end short read data of non-segmented viral genomes

workflows.workflowDescription: Workflow for variant analysis against a reference genome in GenBank format
workflows.workflowName: Paired end variant calling in haploid system

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflow performs variant and genotype calling on whole-genome paired-end sequencing data from organisms of any ploidy to produce annotated VCF and TSV files
workflows.workflowName: Paired end variant and ploidy-aware genotype calling

workflows.workflowDescription: The workflow for Illumina-sequenced ARTIC data builds on the RNASeq workflow for paired-end data using the same steps for mapping and variant calling, but adds extra logic for trimming ARTIC primer sequences off reads with the ivar package. In addition, this workflow uses ivar also to identify amplicons affected by ARTIC primer-binding site mutations and tries to exclude reads derived from such tainted amplicons when calculating allele-frequencies of other variants.
workflows.workflowName: COVID-19: variation analysis on ARTIC PE data

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflows performs paired end read mapping with bwa-mem followed by sensitive variant calling across a wide range of AFs with lofreq
workflows.workflowName: COVID-19: variation analysis on WGS PE data

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflows performs single end read mapping with bowtie2 followed by sensitive variant calling across a wide range of AFs with lofreq
workflows.workflowName: COVID-19: variation analysis on WGS SE data

description: Analyze bulk or single-cell RNA seq data using a variety of approaches.
name: Transcriptomics

workflows.workflowDescription: Comprehensive preprocessing for 10X Genomics CellPlex multiplexed single-cell RNA-seq data. Processes Cell Multiplexing Oligo (CMO) FASTQ files with CITE-seq-Count including required CellPlex-specific translation steps. Simultaneously processes gene expression FASTQ files with STARsolo and DropletUtils for alignment and cell filtering, and formats outputs for seamless import into Seurat/Scanpy (Read10X function).
workflows.workflowName: Single-Cell RNA-seq Preprocessing: 10X Genomics CellPlex Multiplexed Samples

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete preprocessing pipeline for 10X Genomics v3 single-cell RNA-seq data. Aligns raw FASTQ files using STARsolo, performs cell calling and quality filtering with DropletUtils, and formats outputs for seamless import into Seurat/Scanpy (Read10X function).
workflows.workflowName: Single-Cell RNA-seq Preprocessing: 10X Genomics v3 to Seurat and Scanpy Compatible Format

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete RNA-Seq analysis for paired-end data: Processes raw FASTQ data through adapter and bad quality removal (fastp), alignment with STAR using ENCODE parameters, gene quantification via multiple methods (STAR and featureCounts), and expression calculation (FPKM with Cufflinks/StringTie, normalized coverage with bedtools). Produces count tables, normalized expression values, and genomic coverage tracks. Supports stranded and unstranded libraries, generating both HTSeq-compatible counts and normalized measures for downstream analysis.
workflows.workflowName: RNA-Seq Analysis: Paired-End Read Processing and Quantification

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete RNA-Seq analysis for single-end data:  Processes raw FASTQ data through adapter and bad quality removal (fastp), alignment with STAR using ENCODE parameters, gene quantification via multiple methods (STAR and featureCounts), and expression calculation (FPKM with Cufflinks/StringTie, normalized coverage with bedtools). Produces count tables, normalized expression values, and genomic coverage tracks. Supports stranded and unstranded libraries, generating both HTSeq-compatible counts and normalized measures for downstream analysis.
workflows.workflowName: RNA-Seq Analysis: Single-End Read Processing and Quantification

description: Workflows for the analysis of ChIP-seq, ATAC-Seq, and beyond.
name: Regulation

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete ATAC-seq analysis pipeline for paired-end reads. Processes raw FASTQ data through adapter and bad quality removal (cutadapt), alignment (Bowtie2 end-to-end), and filtering (removes MT reads, discordant pairs, low mapping quality <30, PCR duplicates). Generates 5' cut site pileups (±100bp), performs peak calling, and quantifies reads in 1kb summit-centered regions. Produces two normalized coverage tracks (per million mapped reads and per million reads in peaks) and fragment length distribution plots for quality assessment.
workflows.workflowName: ATAC-seq Analysis: Chromatin Accessibility Profiling

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete ChIP-seq analysis for paired-end sequencing data. Processes raw FASTQ files through adapter removal (fastp), alignment to reference genome (Bowtie2), and stringent quality filtering (MAPQ greater than 30, concordant pairs only). Peak calling with MACS2 optimized for paired-end reads identifies protein-DNA binding sites. Generates alignment files, coverage, peak calls, and quality metrics for downstream analysis.
workflows.workflowName: ChIP-seq Analysis: Paired-End Read Processing

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete ChIP-seq analysis for single-end sequencing data. Processes raw FASTQ files through adapter removal (fastp), alignment to reference genome (Bowtie2), and quality filtering (MAPQ greater than 30). Peak calling with MACS2 uses a fixed extension of 200bp to identify protein-DNA binding sites. Generates alignment files, coverage, peak calls, and quality metrics for downstream analysis.
workflows.workflowName: ChIP-seq Analysis: Single-End Read Processing

workflows.workflowDescription: Identifies high-confidence consensus peaks from ATAC-seq or CUT&RUN replicate experiments. The workflow calls peaks on individual replicates and identifies their intersection. To control for sequencing depth differences, it subsamples all replicates to the smallest library size, performs peak calling on the combined normalized data, and retains only peaks whose summits overlap with intersections from a user-defined minimum number of replicates.
workflows.workflowName: Consensus Peak Calling for ATAC-seq and CUT&RUN Replicates

workflows.workflowDescription: Complete CUT&RUN/CUT&TAG analysis workflow for paired-end sequencing data. Processes raw FASTQ files through adapter removal (cutadapt) and alignment (Bowtie2 with dovetail option enabled). Applies quality filtering (MAPQ ≥ 30, concordant pairs only), converts BAM to BED format, and performs peak calling using MACS2 with parameters optimized for the punctate signal profile characteristic of CUT&RUN/CUT&TAG experiments.
workflows.workflowName: CUT&RUN/CUT&TAG Analysis: Protein-DNA Interaction Mapping

workflows.workflowDescription: End-to-end processing of Capture Hi-C paired-end sequencing data. Transforms raw FASTQ files into balanced contact matrices using HiCUP for processing Hi-C reads (adapter trimming, mapping, filtering for valid pairs) with additional filtering for MAPQ and captured regions. The workflow generates sorted tabix files via cooler and produces balanced cool files at user-specified resolution for downstream analysis and visualization.
workflows.workflowName: Capture Hi-C Processing: FASTQ to Balanced Cool Files

workflows.workflowDescription: Comprehensive Hi-C data processing workflow that converts paired-end FASTQ files to balanced contact matrices. Uses HiCUP for pre-processing (adapter trimming, mapping, filtering) with fragment midpoint coordinate assignment, filters reads by mapping quality, and generates sorted tabix files. The final output is a balanced cool file at user-specified resolution created with cooler, ready for multi-resolution contact matrix analysis.
workflows.workflowName: Hi-C Processing: FASTQ to Balanced Cool Files

workflows.workflowDescription: Processes Hi-C paired-end FASTQ files to generate validated interaction pairs using HiCUP. The workflow truncates reads at ligation junctions, maps to reference genome, assigns to restriction fragments, and filters out experimental artifacts (self-ligated, dangling ends, internal fragments, and size outliers). Removes duplicates and converts outputs to formats compatible with Juicebox/cooler using fragment midpoints as coordinates. Final filtering by mapping quality ensures high-confidence interaction data.
workflows.workflowName: Hi-C Data Processing: FASTQ to Valid Interaction Pairs

description: Build consensus sequences for related isolates.
name: Consensus sequences

workflows.workflowDescription: Variant calling and consensus sequence generation for batches of Illumina PE sequenced viruses with uncomplicated and stable genome structure (like e.g. Morbilliviruses).
workflows.workflowName: Variant calling and consensus construction from paired end short read data of non-segmented viral genomes

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflow performs subtyping and consensus sequence generation for batches of Illumina PE sequenced Influenza A isolates.
workflows.workflowName: Influenza A isolate subtyping and consensus sequence generation

workflows.workflowDescription: A workflow for the analysis of pox virus genomes sequenced as half-genomes (for ITR resolution) in a tiled-amplicon approach
workflows.workflowName: Pox Virus Illumina Amplicon Workflow from half-genomes

description: Workflows for the annotation of genomic data.
name: Annotation

workflows.workflowDescription: Antimicrobial resistance gene detection from assembled bacterial genomes
workflows.workflowName: AMR Gene Detection

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflow performs genome annotation using Braker3 and evaluates the quality of the annotation with BUSCO and genome annotation statistics.
workflows.workflowName: Genome annotation with Braker3

workflows.workflowDescription: Annotation of an assembled bacterial genomes to detect genes, potential plasmids, integrons and Insertion sequence (IS) elements.
workflows.workflowName: Bacterial Genome Annotation

workflows.workflowDescription: This workflow runs the FEELnc tool to annotate long non-coding RNAs. Before annotating these long non-coding RNAs, StringTie will be used to assemble the RNA-seq alignments into potential trancriptions. The gffread tool provides a genome annotation file in GTF format.
workflows.workflowName: lncRNAs annotation workflow

description: Assemble prokaryotic and eukaryotic genomes sequenced with a variety of technologies.
name: Assembly

description: Workflows for creation of pairwise and multiple genome alignments.
name: Genome comparisons

description: Analysis of protein folding using the ColabFold framework.
name: Protein folding
